    57→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
    58→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', projectPath)
    59→    // Also set custom type so TiledTerminalView can detect sidebar project drags
    60→    e.dataTransfer.setData('application/x-sidebar-project', projectPath)
    61→  }, [])
    62→
    63→  const handleProjectDragEnd = useCallback(() => {
    64→    setDraggedProject(null)
    65→    setDropTarget(null)
    66→    dropTargetRef.current = null
    67→  }, [])
    68→
    69→  const handleCategoryDragOver = useCallback(
    70→    (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => {
    71→      e.preventDefault()
    72→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
    73→      if (draggedProject) {
    74→        const target: DropTarget = {
    75→          type: categoryId ? 'category' : 'uncategorized',
    76→          id: categoryId,
    77→        }
    78→        setDropTarget(target)
    79→        dropTargetRef.current = target
    80→      }
    81→    },
    82→    [draggedProject]
    83→  )
    84→
    85→  const handleCategoryDrop = useCallback(
    86→    (e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string | null) => {
    87→      e.preventDefault()
    88→      if (draggedProject) {
    89→        moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, categoryId)
    90→      }
    91→      setDraggedProject(null)
    92→      setDropTarget(null)
    93→      dropTargetRef.current = null
    94→    },
    95→    [draggedProject, moveProjectToCategory]
    96→  )
    97→
    98→  const handleProjectDragOver = useCallback(
    99→    (e: React.DragEvent, projectPath: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => {
   100→      e.preventDefault()
   101→      e.stopPropagation()
   102→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
   103→      if (draggedProject && draggedProject !== projectPath) {
   104→        const target: DropTarget = { type: 'project', id: projectPath, position }
   105→        setDropTarget(target)
   106→        dropTargetRef.current = target
   107→      }
   108→    },
   109→    [draggedProject]
   110→  )
   111→
   112→  const handleProjectDrop = useCallback(
   113→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetPath: string, targetCategoryId: string | undefined) => {
   114→      e.preventDefault()
   115→      e.stopPropagation()
   116→
   117→      if (draggedProject && draggedProject !== targetPath) {
   118→        const draggedProjectData = projects.find((p) => p.path === draggedProject)
   119→        const targetProject = projects.find((p) => p.path === targetPath)
   120→
   121→        if (draggedProjectData && targetProject) {
   122→          moveProjectToCategory(draggedProject, targetCategoryId ?? null)
   123→          const categoryProjects = projects
   124→            .filter((p) =>
   125→              targetCategoryId ? p.categoryId === targetCategoryId : !p.categoryId
   126→            )
   127→            .sort((a, b) => (a.order ?? 0) - (b.order ?? 0))
   128→            .map((p) => p.path)
   129→
   130→          const newOrder = categoryProjects.filter((p) => p !== draggedProject)
   131→          const targetIndex = newOrder.indexOf(targetPath)
   132→          // Read from ref to get the latest position (state may be stale)
   133→          const currentDropTarget = dropTargetRef.current
   134→          const insertIndex = currentDropTarget?.position === 'after' ? targetIndex + 1 : targetIndex
   135→          newOrder.splice(insertIndex, 0, draggedProject)
   136→          reorderProjects(targetCategoryId ?? null, newOrder)
   137→        }
   138→      }
   139→      setDraggedProject(null)
   140→      setDropTarget(null)
   141→      dropTargetRef.current = null
   142→    },
   143→    [draggedProject, projects, moveProjectToCategory, reorderProjects]
   144→  )
   145→
   146→  const handleCategoryDragStart = useCallback((e: React.DragEvent, categoryId: string) => {
   147→    setDraggedCategory(categoryId)
   148→    e.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = 'move'
   149→    e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', categoryId)
   150→  }, [])
   151→
   152→  const handleCategoryDragEnd = useCallback(() => {
   153→    setDraggedCategory(null)
   154→    setDropTarget(null)
   155→    dropTargetRef.current = null
   156→  }, [])
   157→
   158→  const handleCategoryHeaderDragOver = useCallback(
   159→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetCategoryId: string, position: 'before' | 'after') => {
   160→      e.preventDefault()
   161→      e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = 'move'
   162→      if (draggedCategory && draggedCategory !== targetCategoryId) {
   163→        const target: DropTarget = { type: 'category', id: targetCategoryId, position }
   164→        setDropTarget(target)
   165→        dropTargetRef.current = target
   166→      }
   167→    },
   168→    [draggedCategory]
   169→  )
   170→
   171→  const handleCategoryHeaderDrop = useCallback(
   172→    (e: React.DragEvent, targetCategoryId: string) => {
   173→      e.preventDefault()
   174→      if (draggedCategory && draggedCategory !== targetCategoryId) {
   175→        const orderedCategories = [...categories].sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order)
   176→        const categoryIds = orderedCategories.map((c) => c.id)
   177→        const newOrder = categoryIds.filter((id) => id !== draggedCategory)
   178→        const targetIndex = newOrder.indexOf(targetCategoryId)
   179→        // Read from ref to get the latest position (state may be stale)
   180→        const currentDropTarget = dropTargetRef.current
   181→        const insertIndex = currentDropTarget?.position === 'after' ? targetIndex + 1 : targetIndex
   182→        newOrder.splice(insertIndex, 0, draggedCategory)
   183→        reorderCategories(newOrder)
   184→      }
   185→      setDraggedCategory(null)
   186→      setDropTarget(null)
   187→      dropTargetRef.current = null
   188→    },
   189→    [draggedCategory, categories, reorderCategories]
   190→  )
   191→
   192→  return {
   193→    draggedProject,
   194→    draggedCategory,
   195→    dropTarget,
   196→    handleProjectDragStart,
   197→    handleProjectDragEnd,
   198→    handleCategoryDragOver,
   199→    handleCategoryDrop,
   200→    handleProjectDragOver,
   201→    handleProjectDrop,
   202→    handleCategoryDragStart,
   203→    handleCategoryDragEnd,
   204→    handleCategoryHeaderDragOver,
   205→    handleCategoryHeaderDrop,
   206→  }
   207→}
   208→
```

/**
 * Electron Backend Implementation
 *
 * This module provides the Electron IPC implementation of the Api interface.
 * It delegates all calls to window.electronAPI which communicates with the
 * Electron main process via IPC.
 */

import {
  Api,
  ExtendedApi,
  Settings,
  Workspace,
  Session,
  PtyDataCallback,
  PtyExitCallback,
  PtyRecreatedCallback,
  ApiOpenSessionCallback,
  Unsubscribe
} from './types'
import type { BackendId } from './types'

/**
 * Type declaration for the global electronAPI
 */
declare global {
  interface Window {
    electronAPI?: {
      // PTY Management
      spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<string>
      killPty: (id: string) => void
      writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
      resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number) => void
      onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void) => () => void
      onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void) => () => void
      onPtyRecreated: (callback: (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: BackendId }) => void) => () => void
      setPtyBackend: (id: string, backend: BackendId) => Promise<void>


      // Session Management
      discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<Session[]>

      // Workspace Management
      getWorkspace: () => Promise<Workspace>
      saveWorkspace: (workspace: Workspace) => Promise<void>

      // Settings Management
      getSettings: () => Promise<Settings>
      saveSettings: (settings: Settings) => Promise<void>

      // Project Management
      addProject: () => Promise<string | null>
      addProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null>

      // TTS
      ttsInstallInstructions: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
      voiceGetInstalled?: () => Promise<Array<{ key: string; displayName: string; source: 'builtin' | 'downloaded' | 'custom'; quality?: string; language?: string }>>
      xttsGetVoices?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; language: string; createdAt: number }>>
      voiceGetSettings?: () => Promise<{ ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }>
      voiceCheckWhisper?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; models: string[]; currentModel: string | null }>
      voiceCheckTTS?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; engine: string | null; voices: string[]; currentVoice: string | null }>
      voiceInstallWhisper?: (model: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
      voiceApplySettings?: (settings: { ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
      voiceSetVoice?: (voice: string | { voice: string; engine: 'piper' | 'xtts' }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
      ttsRemoveInstructions?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
      extensionsGetInstalled?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; type: string }>>
      voiceSpeak: (text: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }>
      voiceStopSpeaking: () => Promise<{ success: boolean }>

      // Events
      onApiOpenSession: (callback: (data: { projectPath: string; autoClose: boolean; model?: string }) => void) => () => void

      // API Server
      apiStart?: (projectPath: string, port: number) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
      apiStop?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>

      // Extended API (Desktop-only)
      selectDirectory: () => Promise<string | null>
      selectExecutable: () => Promise<string | null>
      windowMinimize: () => void
      windowMaximize: () => void
      windowClose: () => void
      windowIsMaximized: () => Promise<boolean>
      getPathForFile: (file: File) => string
      readClipboardImage: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; hasImage?: boolean; path?: string; error?: string }>
      getVersion: () => Promise<string>
      isDebugMode: () => Promise<boolean>
      refresh: () => Promise<void>
      openExternal: (url: string) => Promise<void>
      debugLog: (message: string) => void

      // Beads
      beadsCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ installed: boolean; initialized: boolean }>
      beadsInit: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
      beadsInstall: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; method?: string; needsPython?: boolean }>
      beadsReady: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; tasks?: unknown[]; error?: string }>
      beadsList: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; tasks?: unknown[]; error?: string }>
      beadsShow: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; task?: unknown; error?: string }>
      beadsCreate: (cwd: string, title: string, description?: string, priority?: number, type?: string, labels?: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; task?: unknown; error?: string }>
      beadsComplete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }>

1→import { useEffect, useRef, useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { useWorkspaceStore } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→import { Theme, getThemeById, applyTheme, themes } from '../themes'
     7→import { cleanupOrphanedBuffers } from '../components/terminal/Terminal'
     8→
     9→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderOptions {
    10→  api: Api
    11→  checkInstallation: () => Promise<void>
    12→  setViewMode: (mode: 'tabs' | 'tiled') => void
    13→  setTileLayout: (layout: any[]) => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderReturn {
    17→  loading: boolean
    18→  currentTheme: Theme
    19→  settings: AppSettings | null
    20→  setCurrentTheme: (theme: Theme) => void
    21→  setSettings: (settings: AppSettings | null) => void
    22→}
    23→
    24→export function useWorkspaceLoader({
    25→  api,
    26→  checkInstallation,
    27→  setViewMode,
    28→  setTileLayout
    29→}: UseWorkspaceLoaderOptions): UseWorkspaceLoaderReturn {
    30→  const {
    31→    setProjects,
    32→    setCategories,
    33→    addTab,
    34→    clearTabs
    35→  } = useWorkspaceStore()
    36→
    37→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    38→  const [currentTheme, setCurrentTheme] = useState<Theme>(themes[0])
    39→  const [settings, setSettings] = useState<AppSettings | null>(null)
    40→  const initRef = useRef(false)
    41→  const hadProjectsRef = useRef(false)
    42→
    43→  // Load workspace on mount and restore tabs
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    // Prevent double initialization from StrictMode
    46→    if (initRef.current) return
    47→    initRef.current = true
    48→
    49→    const loadWorkspace = async () => {
    50→      try {
    51→        // Check if Claude, npm, and git-bash are installed
    52→        await checkInstallation()
    53→
    54→        // Load and apply theme with any saved customizations
    55→        const loadedSettings = await api.getSettings()
    56→        setSettings(loadedSettings)
    57→        const theme = getThemeById(loadedSettings.theme || 'default')
    58→        applyTheme(theme, loadedSettings.themeCustomization)
    59→        setCurrentTheme(theme)
    60→
    61→        // Kill any existing PTYs from hot reload (but don't clear buffers - they'll be restored)
    62→        const existingTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    63→        for (const tab of existingTabs) {
    64→          api.killPty(tab.id)
    65→        }
    66→        clearTabs()
    67→
    68→        const workspace = await api.getWorkspace()
    69→        if (workspace.projects) {
    70→          setProjects(workspace.projects)
    71→          if (workspace.projects.length > 0) {
    72→            hadProjectsRef.current = true
    73→          }
    74→          // Install TTS instructions for all existing projects
    75→          for (const project of workspace.projects) {
    76→            await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(project.path)
    77→          }
    78→        }
    79→        // Load categories
    80→        if (workspace.categories) {
    81→          setCategories(workspace.categories)
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        // Restore previously open tabs (only if not already open)
    85→        // Track old ID -> new ID mapping for layout restoration
    86→        const idMapping = new Map<string, string>()
    87→
    88→        if (workspace.openTabs && workspace.openTabs.length > 0) {
    89→          const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    90→          const usedSessionIds = new Set(currentTabs.map(t => t.sessionId).filter(Boolean))
    91→          const sessionsCache = new Map<string, { list: { sessionId: string; slug: string }[]; nextIndex: number }>()
    92→
    93→          for (const savedTab of workspace.openTabs) {
    94→            try {
    95→              // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    96→              await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(savedTab.projectPath)
    97→
    98→              const projectName = savedTab.projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || savedTab.projectPath
    99→              let titleToRestore = savedTab.title || `${projectName} - New`
   100→
   101→              // Get project and determine effective backend for restored tab
   102→              const projectForTab = workspace.projects?.find((p: { path: string }) => p.path === savedTab.projectPath)
   103→              const savedBackend = savedTab.backend
   104→              const effectiveBackendForTab = (savedBackend
   105→                || (projectForTab?.backend && projectForTab.backend !== 'default'
   106→                  ? projectForTab.backend
   107→                  : (loadedSettings?.backend && loadedSettings.backend !== 'default'
   108→                    ? loadedSettings.backend
   109→                    : 'claude'))) as BackendId
   110→
   111→              let sessionIdToRestore: string | undefined = savedTab.sessionId
   112→
   113→              // Always discover sessions to find the most recent one
   114→              let sessionsForProject = sessionsCache.get(savedTab.projectPath)
   115→              if (!sessionsForProject) {
   116→                const list = await api.discoverSessions(savedTab.projectPath, effectiveBackendForTab)
   117→                sessionsForProject = { list, nextIndex: 0 }
   118→                sessionsCache.set(savedTab.projectPath, sessionsForProject)
   119→              }
   120→
   121→              const list = sessionsForProject.list || []
   122→              for (let i = sessionsForProject.nextIndex; i < list.length; i++) {
   123→                const candidate = list[i]
   124→                if (!usedSessionIds.has(candidate.sessionId)) {
   125→                  sessionIdToRestore = candidate.sessionId
   126→                  titleToRestore = `${projectName} - ${candidate.slug}`
   127→                  sessionsForProject.nextIndex = i + 1
   128→                  break
   129→                }
   130→              }
   131→
   132→              const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(
   133→                savedTab.projectPath,
   134→                sessionIdToRestore,
   135→                undefined,
   136→                effectiveBackendForTab
   137→              )
   138→              if (savedTab.id) {
   139→                idMapping.set(savedTab.id, ptyId)
   140→              }
   141→
   142→              addTab({
   143→                id: ptyId,
   144→                projectPath: savedTab.projectPath,
   145→                sessionId: sessionIdToRestore,
   146→                title: titleToRestore,
   147→                ptyId,
   148→                backend: effectiveBackendForTab
   149→              })
   150→              if (sessionIdToRestore) {
   151→                usedSessionIds.add(sessionIdToRestore)
   152→              }
   153→            } catch (e) {
   154→              console.error('Failed to restore tab:', savedTab.projectPath, e)
   155→            }
   156→          }
   157→        }
   158→
   159→        // Clean up orphaned terminal buffers from previous session/HMR
   160→        const activeTabIds = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.map(t => t.id)
   161→        cleanupOrphanedBuffers(activeTabIds)
   162→
   163→        // Restore view mode
   164→        if (workspace.viewMode) {
   165→          setViewMode(workspace.viewMode)
   166→        }
   167→        // Restore tileLayout with mapped IDs and migrate legacy format
   168→        if (workspace.tileLayout && workspace.tileLayout.length > 0 && idMapping.size > 0) {
   169→          const mappedLayout = workspace.tileLayout
   170→            .map(tile => {
   171→              // Migrate legacy tiles: add tabIds/activeTabId if missing
   172→              const tabIds = tile.tabIds || [tile.id]
   173→              const mappedTabIds = tabIds
   174→                .map(id => idMapping.get(id) || id)
   175→                .filter(id => {
   176→                  // Only keep tab IDs that exist in the current session
   177→                  const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
   178→                  return currentTabs.some(t => t.id === id)
   179→                })
   180→
   181→              if (mappedTabIds.length === 0) return null
   182→
   183→              const newId = idMapping.get(tile.id) || mappedTabIds[0]
   184→              const activeTabId = tile.activeTabId
   185→                ? (idMapping.get(tile.activeTabId) || mappedTabIds[0])
   186→                : mappedTabIds[0]
   187→
   188→              return {
   189→                ...tile,
   190→                id: newId,
   191→                tabIds: mappedTabIds,
   192→                activeTabId
   193→              }
   194→            })
   195→            .filter((tile): tile is NonNullable<typeof tile> => tile !== null)
   196→
   197→          if (mappedLayout.length > 0) {
   198→            setTileLayout(mappedLayout)
   199→          }
   200→        }
   201→      } catch (e) {
   202→        console.error('Failed to load workspace:', e)
   203→      }
   204→      setLoading(false)
   205→    }
   206→    loadWorkspace()
   207→  }, [api, addTab, clearTabs, checkInstallation, setProjects, setCategories, setViewMode, setTileLayout])
   208→
   209→  return {
   210→    loading,
   211→    currentTheme,
   212→    settings,
   213→    setCurrentTheme,
   214→    setSettings
   215→  }
   216→}
   217→

1→import { useCallback, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { OpenTab, Project } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→import { TileLayout, DropZone, splitTile, addTileToLayout, addTabToExistingTile } from '../components/TiledTerminalView'
     7→import { clearTerminalBuffer } from '../components/terminal/Terminal'
     8→
     9→interface UseProjectHandlersOptions {
    10→  api: Api
    11→  projects: Project[]
    12→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    13→  settings: AppSettings | null
    14→  tileLayout: TileLayout[]
    15→  addProject: (project: { path: string; name: string }) => void
    16→  removeTab: (id: string) => void
    17→  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
    18→  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
    19→  setTileLayout: (layout: TileLayout[]) => void
    20→}
    21→
    22→interface ClosedTabInfo {
    23→  projectPath: string
    24→  sessionId?: string
    25→  title: string
    26→  backend?: string
    27→}
    28→
    29→interface UseProjectHandlersReturn {
    30→  handleAddProject: () => Promise<void>
    31→  handleAddProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<void>
    32→  handleOpenSession: (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, initialPrompt?: string, forceNewSession?: boolean) => Promise<void>
    33→  handleOpenSessionAtPosition: (projectPath: string, dropZone: DropZone | null, containerSize: { width: number; height: number }, currentLayout?: TileLayout[]) => Promise<void>
    34→  handleCloseTab: (tabId: string) => void
    35→  handleCloseProjectTabs: (projectPath: string) => void
    36→  handleProjectCreated: (projectPath: string, projectName: string) => void
    37→  handleUndoCloseTab: () => void
    38→  canUndoCloseTab: boolean
    39→}
    40→
    41→export function useProjectHandlers({
    42→  api,
    43→  projects,
    44→  openTabs,
    45→  settings,
    46→  tileLayout,
    47→  addProject,
    48→  removeTab,
    49→  addTab,
    50→  setActiveTab,
    51→  setTileLayout
    52→}: UseProjectHandlersOptions): UseProjectHandlersReturn {
    53→  const closedTabsRef = useRef<ClosedTabInfo[]>([])
    54→  const [closedTabCount, setClosedTabCount] = useState(0)
    55→
    56→  // Keep a ref to the latest tileLayout so async callbacks always read the freshest state
    57→  const tileLayoutRef = useRef(tileLayout)
    58→  tileLayoutRef.current = tileLayout
    59→
    60→  const handleAddProject = useCallback(async () => {
    61→    const path = await api.addProject()
    62→    if (path) {
    63→      const name = path.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || path
    64→      addProject({ path, name })
    65→      await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(path)
    66→    }
    67→  }, [api, addProject])
    68→
    69→  const handleAddProjectsFromParent = useCallback(async () => {
    70→    const projectsToAdd = await api.addProjectsFromParent?.()
    71→    if (projectsToAdd && projectsToAdd.length > 0) {
    72→      const existingPaths = new Set(projects.map((p) => p.path))
    73→      const newProjects = projectsToAdd.filter((p) => !existingPaths.has(p.path))
    74→
    75→      for (const project of newProjects) {
    76→        addProject({ path: project.path, name: project.name })
    77→        await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(project.path)
    78→      }
    79→    }
    80→  }, [api, addProject, projects])
    81→
    82→  const handleOpenSession = useCallback(async (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, initialPrompt?: string, forceNewSession?: boolean) => {
    83→    // Check if this session is already open
    84→    if (sessionId) {
    85→      const existingTab = openTabs.find(tab => tab.sessionId === sessionId)
    86→      if (existingTab) {
    87→        setActiveTab(existingTab.id)
    88→        return
    89→      }
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    // Get project and determine effective backend
    93→    const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    94→    const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    95→      ? project.backend
    96→      : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
    97→        ? settings.backend
    98→        : 'claude')) as BackendId
    99→
   100→    // Only discover sessions if no specific sessionId was requested
   101→    if (!forceNewSession && !sessionId) {
   102→      try {
   103→        const sessions = await api.discoverSessions(projectPath, effectiveBackend)
   104→        if (sessions.length > 0) {
   105→          const [mostRecent] = sessions
   106→          const existingTab = openTabs.find((tab) => tab.sessionId === mostRecent.sessionId)
   107→
   108→          if (existingTab) {
   109→            setActiveTab(existingTab.id)
   110→            return
   111→          }
   112→          sessionId = mostRecent.sessionId
   113→          slug = mostRecent.slug
   114→        }
   115→      } catch (e) {
   116→        console.error('Failed to discover sessions for project:', e)
   117→      }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    const projectName = projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath
   121→    const title = slug ? `${projectName} - ${slug}` : `${projectName} - New`
   122→
   123→    try {
   124→      await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
   125→
   126→      const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(projectPath, sessionId, undefined, effectiveBackend)
   127→
   128→      // Compute tile layout BEFORE adding tab so both update together,
   129→      // preventing useEffectiveLayout from auto-adding at the wrong position
   130→      const currentLayout = tileLayoutRef.current
   131→      const newLayout = addTileToLayout(currentLayout, ptyId, null, window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
   132→
   133→      addTab({
   134→        id: ptyId,
   135→        projectPath,
   136→        sessionId,
   137→        title,
   138→        ptyId,
   139→        backend: effectiveBackend
   140→      })
   141→      setTileLayout(newLayout)
   142→
   143→      // If an initial prompt was provided, send it after a short delay
   144→      if (initialPrompt) {
   145→        setTimeout(() => {
   146→          api.writePty(ptyId, initialPrompt)
   147→          setTimeout(() => {
   148→            api.writePty(ptyId, '\r')
   149→          }, 100)
   150→        }, 1500)

     1→import { useCallback, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { OpenTab, Project } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→import { TileLayout, DropZone, splitTile, addTileToLayout, addTabToExistingTile } from '../components/TiledTerminalView'
     7→import { clearTerminalBuffer } from '../components/terminal/Terminal'
     8→
     9→interface UseProjectHandlersOptions {
    10→  api: Api
    11→  projects: Project[]
    12→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    13→  settings: AppSettings | null
    14→  tileLayout: TileLayout[]
    15→  addProject: (project: { path: string; name: string }) => void
    16→  removeTab: (id: string) => void
    17→  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
    18→  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
    19→  setTileLayout: (layout: TileLayout[]) => void
    20→}
    21→

    23→  projectPath: string

    25→  title: string
    26→  backend?: string
    27→}
    28→
    29→interface UseProjectHandlersReturn {
    30→  handleAddProject: () => Promise<void>
    31→  handleAddProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<void>
    32→  handleOpenSession: (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, initialPrompt?: string, forceNewSession?: boolean) => Promise<void>
    33→  handleOpenSessionAtPosition: (projectPath: string, dropZone: DropZone | null, containerSize: { width: number; height: number }, currentLayout?: TileLayout[]) => Promise<void>
    34→  handleCloseTab: (tabId: string) => void
    35→  handleCloseProjectTabs: (projectPath: string) => void
    36→  handleProjectCreated: (projectPath: string, projectName: string) => void
    37→  handleUndoCloseTab: () => void
    38→  canUndoCloseTab: boolean
    39→}
    40→
    41→export function useProjectHandlers({
    42→  api,
    43→  projects,
    44→  openTabs,
    45→  settings,
    46→  tileLayout,
    47→  addProject,
    48→  removeTab,
    49→  addTab,
    50→  setActiveTab,
    51→  setTileLayout
    52→}: UseProjectHandlersOptions): UseProjectHandlersReturn {
    53→  const closedTabsRef = useRef<ClosedTabInfo[]>([])
    54→  const [closedTabCount, setClosedTabCount] = useState(0)
    55→
    56→  // Keep a ref to the latest tileLayout so async callbacks always read the freshest state
    57→  const tileLayoutRef = useRef(tileLayout)
    58→  tileLayoutRef.current = tileLayout
    59→
    60→  const handleAddProject = useCallback(async () => {
    61→    const path = await api.addProject()
    62→    if (path) {
    63→      const name = path.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || path
    64→      addProject({ path, name })
    65→      await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(path)
    66→    }
    67→  }, [api, addProject])
    68→
    69→  const handleAddProjectsFromParent = useCallback(async () => {
    70→    const projectsToAdd = await api.addProjectsFromParent?.()
    71→    if (projectsToAdd && projectsToAdd.length > 0) {
    72→      const existingPaths = new Set(projects.map((p) => p.path))
    73→      const newProjects = projectsToAdd.filter((p) => !existingPaths.has(p.path))
    74→
    75→      for (const project of newProjects) {
    76→        addProject({ path: project.path, name: project.name })
    77→        await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(project.path)
    78→      }
    79→    }
    80→  }, [api, addProject, projects])
    81→
    82→  const handleOpenSession = useCallback(async (projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, slug?: string, initialPrompt?: string, forceNewSession?: boolean) => {
    83→    // Check if this session is already open
    84→    if (sessionId) {
    85→      const existingTab = openTabs.find(tab => tab.sessionId === sessionId)
    86→      if (existingTab) {
    87→        setActiveTab(existingTab.id)
    88→        return
    89→      }
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    // Get project and determine effective backend
    93→    const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    94→    const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    95→      ? project.backend
    96→      : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
    97→        ? settings.backend
    98→        : 'claude')) as BackendId
    99→
   100→    // Only discover sessions if no specific sessionId was requested
   101→    if (!forceNewSession && !sessionId) {
   102→      try {
   103→        const sessions = await api.discoverSessions(projectPath, effectiveBackend)
   104→        if (sessions.length > 0) {
   105→          const [mostRecent] = sessions

   107→
   108→          if (existingTab) {
   109→            setActiveTab(existingTab.id)
   110→            return
   111→          }
   112→          sessionId = mostRecent.sessionId
   113→          slug = mostRecent.slug
   114→        }

   117→      }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    const projectName = projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath
   121→    const title = slug ? `${projectName} - ${slug}` : `${projectName} - New`
   122→
   123→    try {
   124→      await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
   125→

   128→      // Compute tile layout BEFORE adding tab so both update together,
   129→      // preventing useEffectiveLayout from auto-adding at the wrong position
   130→      const currentLayout = tileLayoutRef.current
   131→      const newLayout = addTileToLayout(currentLayout, ptyId, null, window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
   132→
   133→      addTab({
   134→        id: ptyId,

   141→      setTileLayout(newLayout)

1→import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { Project } from '../../../stores/workspace.js'
     3→import { ProjectSettingsModalState, InstalledVoice } from '../types.js'
     4→import { COMMON_TOOLS } from '../constants.js'
     5→
     6→interface UseProjectSettingsModalOptions {
     7→  onUpdateProject: (path: string, updates: Partial<Project>) => void
     8→}
     9→
    10→interface UseProjectSettingsModalReturn {
    11→  projectSettingsModal: ProjectSettingsModalState | null
    12→  installedVoices: InstalledVoice[]
    13→  globalVoiceSettings: { voice: string; engine: string }
    14→  globalPermissions: { tools: string[]; mode: string }
    15→  apiStatus: Record<string, { running: boolean; port?: number }>
    16→  setApiStatus: React.Dispatch<
    17→    React.SetStateAction<Record<string, { running: boolean; port?: number }>>
    18→  >
    19→  handleOpenProjectSettings: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    20→  handleSaveProjectSettings: () => Promise<void>
    21→  handleToggleApi: (project: Project) => Promise<void>
    22→  setProjectSettingsModal: React.Dispatch<
    23→    React.SetStateAction<ProjectSettingsModalState | null>
    24→  >
    25→  handleToggleTool: (tool: string) => void
    26→  handleAllowAll: () => void
    27→  handleClearAll: () => void
    28→  handleProjectSettingsChange: (updates: Partial<ProjectSettingsModalState>) => void
    29→}
    30→
    31→export function useProjectSettingsModal({
    32→  onUpdateProject,
    33→}: UseProjectSettingsModalOptions): UseProjectSettingsModalReturn {
    34→  const [projectSettingsModal, setProjectSettingsModal] =
    35→    useState<ProjectSettingsModalState | null>(null)
    36→  const [installedVoices, setInstalledVoices] = useState<InstalledVoice[]>([])
    37→  const [globalVoiceSettings, setGlobalVoiceSettings] = useState<{
    38→    voice: string
    39→    engine: string
    40→  }>({ voice: '', engine: '' })
    41→  const [globalPermissions, setGlobalPermissions] = useState<{
    42→    tools: string[]
    43→    mode: string
    44→  }>({ tools: [], mode: 'default' })
    45→  const [apiStatus, setApiStatus] = useState<
    46→    Record<string, { running: boolean; port?: number }>
    47→  >({})
    48→
    49→  const handleOpenProjectSettings = useCallback(async (project: Project) => {
    50→    const settings = await window.electronAPI?.getSettings()
    51→    setGlobalPermissions({
    52→      tools: settings?.autoAcceptTools || [],
    53→      mode: settings?.permissionMode || 'default',
    54→    })
    55→
    56→    try {
    57→      const [piperVoices, xttsVoices, voiceSettings] = await Promise.all([
    58→        (window.electronAPI?.voiceGetInstalled?.() || []) as InstalledVoice[],
    59→        window.electronAPI?.xttsGetVoices?.() || [],
    60→        window.electronAPI?.voiceGetSettings?.() || {},
    61→      ])
    62→      const combined: InstalledVoice[] = []
    63→      if (piperVoices) combined.push(...piperVoices)
    64→      if (xttsVoices) {
    65→        combined.push(
    66→          ...xttsVoices.map((v: { id: string; name: string }) => ({
    67→            key: v.id,
    68→            displayName: v.name,
    69→            source: 'xtts',
    70→          }))
    71→        )
    72→      }
    73→      setInstalledVoices(combined)
    74→      const voice = (voiceSettings as { ttsVoice?: string }).ttsVoice || ''
    75→      const engine = (voiceSettings as { ttsEngine?: string }).ttsEngine || 'piper'
    76→      setGlobalVoiceSettings({ voice, engine })
    77→    } catch (e) {
    78→      console.error('Failed to load voice settings:', e)
    79→      setInstalledVoices([])
    80→      setGlobalVoiceSettings({ voice: '', engine: 'piper' })
    81→    }
    82→
    83→    setProjectSettingsModal({
    84→      project,
    85→      apiPort: project.apiPort?.toString() || '',
    86→      apiAutoStart: project.apiAutoStart || false,
    87→      apiSessionMode: project.apiSessionMode || 'existing',
    88→      apiModel: project.apiModel || 'default',
    89→      tools: project.autoAcceptTools || [],
    90→      permissionMode: project.permissionMode || 'default',
    91→      ttsVoice: project.ttsVoice || '',
    92→      ttsEngine: project.ttsEngine || '',
    93→      backend: project.backend || 'default',
    94→    })
    95→  }, [])
    96→
    97→  const handleSaveProjectSettings = useCallback(async () => {
    98→    if (!projectSettingsModal) return
    99→
   100→    const port = parseInt(projectSettingsModal.apiPort, 10)
   101→    const hasPortValue = projectSettingsModal.apiPort.trim() !== ''
   102→
   103→    if (hasPortValue && (isNaN(port) || port < 1024 || port > 65535)) {
   104→      setProjectSettingsModal({
   105→        ...projectSettingsModal,
   106→        apiStatus: 'error',
   107→        apiError: 'Please enter a valid port number (1024-65535)',
   108→      })
   109→      return
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    const newPort = hasPortValue ? port : undefined
   113→    const oldPort = projectSettingsModal.project.apiPort
   114→
   115→    if (newPort !== oldPort) {
   116→      if (!newPort) {
   117→        await window.electronAPI?.apiStop?.(projectSettingsModal.project.path)
   118→        setApiStatus((prev) => ({
   119→          ...prev,
   120→          [projectSettingsModal.project.path]: { running: false },
   121→        }))
   122→      } else {
   123→        setProjectSettingsModal({ ...projectSettingsModal, apiStatus: 'checking' })
   124→        const result = await window.electronAPI?.apiStart?.(
   125→          projectSettingsModal.project.path,
   126→          newPort
   127→        )
   128→        if (!result?.success) {
   129→          setProjectSettingsModal({
   130→            ...projectSettingsModal,
   131→            apiStatus: 'error',
   132→            apiError: result?.error || 'Port may already be in use',
   133→          })
   134→          return
   135→        }
   136→        setApiStatus((prev) => ({
   137→          ...prev,
   138→          [projectSettingsModal.project.path]: { running: true, port: newPort },
   139→        }))
   140→      }
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    onUpdateProject(projectSettingsModal.project.path, {
   144→      apiPort: newPort,
   145→      apiAutoStart: projectSettingsModal.apiAutoStart || undefined,
   146→      apiSessionMode:
   147→        projectSettingsModal.apiSessionMode !== 'existing'
   148→          ? projectSettingsModal.apiSessionMode
   149→          : undefined,
   150→      apiModel:
   151→        projectSettingsModal.apiModel !== 'default'
   152→          ? projectSettingsModal.apiModel
   153→          : undefined,
   154→      autoAcceptTools:
   155→        projectSettingsModal.tools.length > 0 ? projectSettingsModal.tools : undefined,
   156→      permissionMode:
   157→        projectSettingsModal.permissionMode !== 'default'
   158→          ? projectSettingsModal.permissionMode
   159→          : undefined,
   160→      ttsVoice: projectSettingsModal.ttsVoice || undefined,
   161→      ttsEngine: projectSettingsModal.ttsEngine || undefined,
   162→      backend:
   163→        projectSettingsModal.backend !== 'default'
   164→          ? projectSettingsModal.backend
   165→          : undefined,
   166→    })
   167→
   168→    setProjectSettingsModal(null)
   169→  }, [projectSettingsModal, onUpdateProject])
   170→
   171→  const handleToggleApi = useCallback(
   172→    async (project: Project) => {
   173→      const status = apiStatus[project.path]
   174→      if (status?.running) {
   175→        await window.electronAPI?.apiStop?.(project.path)
   176→        setApiStatus((prev) => ({ ...prev, [project.path]: { running: false } }))
   177→      } else if (project.apiPort) {
   178→        const result = await window.electronAPI?.apiStart?.(project.path, project.apiPort)
   179→        if (result?.success) {
   180→          setApiStatus((prev) => ({
   181→            ...prev,
   182→            [project.path]: { running: true, port: project.apiPort },
   183→          }))
   184→        } else {
   185→          alert(`Failed to start API server: ${result?.error || 'Unknown error'}`)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→    },
   189→    [apiStatus]
   190→  )
   191→
   192→  const handleToggleTool = useCallback((tool: string) => {
   193→    setProjectSettingsModal((prev) => {
   194→      if (!prev) return null
   195→      const newTools = prev.tools.includes(tool)
   196→        ? prev.tools.filter((t) => t !== tool)
   197→        : [...prev.tools, tool]
   198→      return { ...prev, tools: newTools }
   199→    })
   200→  }, [])

Thoroughly explore the codebase at /home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal for bugs related to "workspace persistence and settings". 

Key files I've already identified:
- src/main/session-store.ts - SessionStore class handles persistence
- src/main/app/ipc-handlers/workspace.ts - workspace IPC handlers
- src/main/app/ipc-handlers/settings.ts - settings IPC handlers

Focus areas for the audit:
1. Race conditions in save/load (concurrent saves, data loss)
2. Settings not persisting correctly (missing fields, type mismatches)
3. Workspace state getting corrupted or lost (empty workspace overwriting populated one - there's a guard but is it sufficient?)
4. Tab/session state not persisting across restarts
5. Category/project ordering not persisting
6. Any other persistence-related bugs

Look at:
- How the renderer calls workspace:save and settings:save
- The meta-project-sync module
- How workspace state flows from renderer to main process
- Any debouncing or batching of saves
- The sidebar components that manage workspace state
- The renderer's API layer (src/renderer/api/index.ts)

Report all potential bugs with file paths and line numbers.

content.text:
Perfect. I have enough information now. Let me compile my findings into a comprehensive audit report.

## Comprehensive Workspace Persistence & Settings Audit Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've identified several bugs and potential issues related to workspace persistence and settings management:

---

### **CRITICAL BUG #1: Type Mismatch in OpenTab Interface**

**Location:** 
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (line 36)
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts` (line 34)

**Issue:** The `ptyId` field has conflicting type definitions:
- **Main process** (`session-store.ts:36`): `ptyId?: string` (optional)
- **Renderer** (`workspace.ts:34`): `ptyId: string` (required)

**Impact:** When the renderer sends `openTabs` to be saved via `api.saveWorkspace()` (MainApp.tsx:187-194), all tabs include `ptyId` values. However, when restoring tabs from disk, the main process's optional definition allows missing `ptyId` values, causing type inconsistency. This can lead to:
- Tabs restored without proper ptyId values
- Runtime errors when trying to access ptyId
- Potential terminal session restoration failures

**Fix needed:** Align the `ptyId` type definition - it should be `ptyId: string` (required) in both places since all open tabs must have a ptyId.

---

### **BUG #2: Race Condition in Empty Workspace Protection**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 214-225)

**Issue:** The empty workspace guard checks `this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0`:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
  console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
  return
}
```

**Problem:** 
- If multiple `saveWorkspace()` calls occur rapidly (as they do in useEffect dependency array), `this.data.workspace` is updated synchronously (line 222: `this.data.workspace = workspace`) BEFORE the actual file write completes.
- A third concurrent save could see the updated `this.data` as "current projects" and allow an empty workspace through
- No mutex/locking mechanism protects against concurrent saves

**Impact:** Potential data loss during rapid workspace mutations (e.g., dragging multiple projects quickly, adding/removing projects in succession).

---

### **BUG #3: Missing Debouncing/Batching on Workspace Saves**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 171-201)

**Issue:** The workspace save effect triggers on EVERY change to: `projects`, `openTabs`, `activeTabId`, `viewMode`, `tileLayout`, or `categories`. This causes:

```typescript
useEffect(() => {
  // ... save logic
  api.saveWorkspace({...})
}, [api, projects, openTabs, activeTabId, loading, viewMode, tileLayout, categories])
```

**Impact:**
- **Rapid successive saves** during drag operations (each drag event over 100ms+ triggers reorders)
- **High IPC traffic** - each render state change triggers a full workspace file write
- **Potential data loss** - if a save-in-progress is interrupted by another save before the temp file rename completes
- **Performance degradation** - synchronous JSON.stringify on large workspaces on every change

**Fix needed:** Implement debouncing (300-500ms) to batch workspace changes before persisting.

---

### **BUG #4: Type Mismatch in Settings Return Value**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 236-237)

**Issue:** 
```typescript
getSettings(): Settings {
  return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
}
```

**Problem:** The fallback object is missing many optional but important settings fields:
- `voiceOutputEnabled`
- `voiceVolume`
- `voiceSpeed`
- `voiceSkipOnNew`
- `voiceSilenceThreshold`
- `themeCustomization`
- `autoAcceptTools`
- `permissionMode`

When `this.data.settings` is undefined (first startup or corrupted file), these fields are lost, and callers might not handle undefined values properly.

**Impact:** Missing voice settings, theme customization, and tool permissions on fresh installs or after corruption.

---

### **BUG #5: Categories Not Persisted with Optional Property**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (line 56)

**Issue:** The `categories` field in the Workspace interface is optional:
```typescript
categories?: ProjectCategory[]
```

**Problem:** 
- When saving workspace (MainApp.tsx:198), `categories` is always included in the save
- But when loading (useWorkspaceLoader.ts:80-81), it only restores if present:
```typescript
if (workspace.categories) {
  setCategories(workspace.categories)
}
```
- If the file is corrupted or doesn't include `categories`, they're silently lost without warning
- No migration path from workspaces without categories to those with them

**Impact:** Category ordering and grouping can be lost on file corruption or upgrade scenarios.

---

### **BUG #6: Project Ordering Lost When Moving Between Categories**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts` (lines 176-189)

**Issue:** The `moveProjectToCategory` function sets new order:
```typescript
moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath, categoryId) => {
  const categoryProjects = projects.filter((p) =>
    categoryId === null ? !p.categoryId : p.categoryId === categoryId
  )
  const maxOrder = categoryProjects.reduce((max, p) => Math.max(max, p.order ?? -1), -1)
  set({
    projects: projects.map((p) =>
      p.path === projectPath
        ? { ...p, categoryId: categoryId ?? undefined, order: maxOrder + 1 }
        : p
    )
  })
}
```

**Problem:** When a project is dragged between categories:
1. First `moveProjectToCategory` is called (sets order = maxOrder + 1)
2. Then `reorderProjects` is called in the same drop handler (useDragAndDrop.ts:136)
3. But if only `moveProjectToCategory` is called without subsequent reorder, the project ends up at the end, potentially reordering other projects unintentionally

**Additional Issue:** The `reorderProjects` function (line 191-201) doesn't validate the `categoryId` parameter - it reorders ALL projects globally regardless of category, which is wrong!

---

### **BUG #7: Insufficient Settings Field Defaults**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 82-94)

**Issue:** The Settings interface marks most fields as optional:
```typescript
voiceOutputEnabled?: boolean
voiceVolume?: number
voiceSpeed?: number
voiceSkipOnNew?: boolean
permissionMode?: string
```

But when not provided, UI components might crash or exhibit default behavior unexpectedly.

**Impact:** Voice settings defaults to undefined rather than sensible values (e.g., volume should default to 100%, speed to 1.0x).

---

### **BUG #8: SessionStorage-Based Hot Reload Guard is Weak**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 173-183)

**Issue:** 
```typescript
const hadProjects = sessionStorage.getItem('hadProjects') === 'true' || hadProjectsRef.current
if (projects.length === 0 && hadProjects) {
  console.warn('Skipping save: projects empty but previously had projects (likely hot reload)')
  return
}
```

**Problem:**
- SessionStorage is cleared on full browser refresh, making this guard ineffective
- The `hadProjectsRef` is only set AFTER first check, so on the very first empty projects load, it won't save but `hadProjectsRef` won't be set yet
- Edge case: If first render has projects, then second render drops to 0 (before ref is set), the save is blocked

**Impact:** First-time empty workspace might not be saved correctly on hot reload scenarios.

---

### **BUG #9: No Validation of Workspace State**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 214-225)

**Issue:** `saveWorkspace()` only checks if `projects.length === 0`, not other invalidity:
- No check for cyclic project references
- No check for orphaned openTabs (referencing non-existent projects)
- No validation that `activeTabId` exists in `openTabs`
- No validation that projects referenced by categories actually exist

**Impact:** Workspace file could contain corrupted references causing runtime errors on load.

---

### **BUG #10: OpenTab Mapping Creates New Objects on Every Save**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 187-194)

**Issue:**
```typescript
openTabs: openTabs.map(t => ({
  id: t.id,
  projectPath: t.projectPath,
  sessionId: t.sessionId,
  title: t.title,
  ptyId: t.ptyId,
  backend: t.backend
}))
```

**Problem:** This creates new objects which might omit fields that exist in the original `OpenTab` but aren't explicitly listed. If `OpenTab` is extended in the future, new fields won't be saved.

**Better approach:** Use spread syntax: `...t` to capture all fields.

---

## Summary of Severity

| Bug | Severity | Category |
|-----|----------|----------|
| #1: OpenTab ptyId type mismatch | **CRITICAL** | Type system / Data loss |
| #2: Race condition in save guard | **HIGH** | Concurrency / Data loss |
| #3: No debouncing on saves | **HIGH** | Performance / Data loss |
| #4: Settings fallback incomplete | **MEDIUM** | Data integrity |
| #5: Categories optional in workspace | **MEDIUM** | Data loss |
| #6: Project reorder logic flawed | **MEDIUM** | User experience |
| #7: Settings field defaults | **LOW** | Code quality |
| #8: SessionStorage guard weak | **LOW** | Edge cases |
| #9: No workspace validation | **LOW** | Robustness |
| #10: OpenTab mapping incomplete | **LOW** | Future-proofing |

---

## File Paths for Reference

Perfect. I have enough information now. Let me compile my findings into a comprehensive audit report.

## Comprehensive Workspace Persistence & Settings Audit Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've identified several bugs and potential issues related to workspace persistence and settings management:

---

### **CRITICAL BUG #1: Type Mismatch in OpenTab Interface**

**Location:** 
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (line 36)
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts` (line 34)

**Issue:** The `ptyId` field has conflicting type definitions:
- **Main process** (`session-store.ts:36`): `ptyId?: string` (optional)
- **Renderer** (`workspace.ts:34`): `ptyId: string` (required)

**Impact:** When the renderer sends `openTabs` to be saved via `api.saveWorkspace()` (MainApp.tsx:187-194), all tabs include `ptyId` values. However, when restoring tabs from disk, the main process's optional definition allows missing `ptyId` values, causing type inconsistency. This can lead to:
- Tabs restored without proper ptyId values
- Runtime errors when trying to access ptyId
- Potential terminal session restoration failures

**Fix needed:** Align the `ptyId` type definition - it should be `ptyId: string` (required) in both places since all open tabs must have a ptyId.

---

### **BUG #2: Race Condition in Empty Workspace Protection**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 214-225)

**Issue:** The empty workspace guard checks `this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0`:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
  console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
  return
}
```

**Problem:** 
- If multiple `saveWorkspace()` calls occur rapidly (as they do in useEffect dependency array), `this.data.workspace` is updated synchronously (line 222: `this.data.workspace = workspace`) BEFORE the actual file write completes.
- A third concurrent save could see the updated `this.data` as "current projects" and allow an empty workspace through
- No mutex/locking mechanism protects against concurrent saves

**Impact:** Potential data loss during rapid workspace mutations (e.g., dragging multiple projects quickly, adding/removing projects in succession).

---

### **BUG #3: Missing Debouncing/Batching on Workspace Saves**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 171-201)

**Issue:** The workspace save effect triggers on EVERY change to: `projects`, `openTabs`, `activeTabId`, `viewMode`, `tileLayout`, or `categories`. This causes:

```typescript
useEffect(() => {
  // ... save logic
  api.saveWorkspace({...})
}, [api, projects, openTabs, activeTabId, loading, viewMode, tileLayout, categories])
```

**Impact:**
- **Rapid successive saves** during drag operations (each drag event over 100ms+ triggers reorders)
- **High IPC traffic** - each render state change triggers a full workspace file write
- **Potential data loss** - if a save-in-progress is interrupted by another save before the temp file rename completes
- **Performance degradation** - synchronous JSON.stringify on large workspaces on every change

**Fix needed:** Implement debouncing (300-500ms) to batch workspace changes before persisting.

---

### **BUG #4: Type Mismatch in Settings Return Value**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 236-237)

**Issue:** 
```typescript
getSettings(): Settings {
  return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
}
```

**Problem:** The fallback object is missing many optional but important settings fields:
- `voiceOutputEnabled`
- `voiceVolume`
- `voiceSpeed`
- `voiceSkipOnNew`
- `voiceSilenceThreshold`
- `themeCustomization`
- `autoAcceptTools`
- `permissionMode`

When `this.data.settings` is undefined (first startup or corrupted file), these fields are lost, and callers might not handle undefined values properly.

**Impact:** Missing voice settings, theme customization, and tool permissions on fresh installs or after corruption.

---

### **BUG #5: Categories Not Persisted with Optional Property**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (line 56)

**Issue:** The `categories` field in the Workspace interface is optional:
```typescript
categories?: ProjectCategory[]
```

**Problem:** 
- When saving workspace (MainApp.tsx:198), `categories` is always included in the save
- But when loading (useWorkspaceLoader.ts:80-81), it only restores if present:
```typescript
if (workspace.categories) {
  setCategories(workspace.categories)
}
```
- If the file is corrupted or doesn't include `categories`, they're silently lost without warning
- No migration path from workspaces without categories to those with them

**Impact:** Category ordering and grouping can be lost on file corruption or upgrade scenarios.

---

### **BUG #6: Project Ordering Lost When Moving Between Categories**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts` (lines 176-189)

**Issue:** The `moveProjectToCategory` function sets new order:
```typescript
moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath, categoryId) => {
  const categoryProjects = projects.filter((p) =>
    categoryId === null ? !p.categoryId : p.categoryId === categoryId
  )
  const maxOrder = categoryProjects.reduce((max, p) => Math.max(max, p.order ?? -1), -1)
  set({
    projects: projects.map((p) =>
      p.path === projectPath
        ? { ...p, categoryId: categoryId ?? undefined, order: maxOrder + 1 }
        : p
    )
  })
}
```

**Problem:** When a project is dragged between categories:
1. First `moveProjectToCategory` is called (sets order = maxOrder + 1)
2. Then `reorderProjects` is called in the same drop handler (useDragAndDrop.ts:136)
3. But if only `moveProjectToCategory` is called without subsequent reorder, the project ends up at the end, potentially reordering other projects unintentionally

**Additional Issue:** The `reorderProjects` function (line 191-201) doesn't validate the `categoryId` parameter - it reorders ALL projects globally regardless of category, which is wrong!

---

### **BUG #7: Insufficient Settings Field Defaults**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 82-94)

**Issue:** The Settings interface marks most fields as optional:
```typescript
voiceOutputEnabled?: boolean
voiceVolume?: number
voiceSpeed?: number
voiceSkipOnNew?: boolean
permissionMode?: string
```

But when not provided, UI components might crash or exhibit default behavior unexpectedly.

**Impact:** Voice settings defaults to undefined rather than sensible values (e.g., volume should default to 100%, speed to 1.0x).

---

### **BUG #8: SessionStorage-Based Hot Reload Guard is Weak**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 173-183)

**Issue:** 
```typescript
const hadProjects = sessionStorage.getItem('hadProjects') === 'true' || hadProjectsRef.current
if (projects.length === 0 && hadProjects) {
  console.warn('Skipping save: projects empty but previously had projects (likely hot reload)')
  return
}
```

**Problem:**
- SessionStorage is cleared on full browser refresh, making this guard ineffective
- The `hadProjectsRef` is only set AFTER first check, so on the very first empty projects load, it won't save but `hadProjectsRef` won't be set yet
- Edge case: If first render has projects, then second render drops to 0 (before ref is set), the save is blocked

**Impact:** First-time empty workspace might not be saved correctly on hot reload scenarios.

---

### **BUG #9: No Validation of Workspace State**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` (lines 214-225)

**Issue:** `saveWorkspace()` only checks if `projects.length === 0`, not other invalidity:
- No check for cyclic project references
- No check for orphaned openTabs (referencing non-existent projects)
- No validation that `activeTabId` exists in `openTabs`
- No validation that projects referenced by categories actually exist

**Impact:** Workspace file could contain corrupted references causing runtime errors on load.

---

### **BUG #10: OpenTab Mapping Creates New Objects on Every Save**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx` (lines 187-194)

**Issue:**
```typescript
openTabs: openTabs.map(t => ({
  id: t.id,
  projectPath: t.projectPath,
  sessionId: t.sessionId,
  title: t.title,
  ptyId: t.ptyId,
  backend: t.backend
}))
```

**Problem:** This creates new objects which might omit fields that exist in the original `OpenTab` but aren't explicitly listed. If `OpenTab` is extended in the future, new fields won't be saved.

**Better approach:** Use spread syntax: `...t` to capture all fields.

---

## Summary of Severity

|-----|----------|----------|
| #1: OpenTab ptyId type mismatch | **CRITICAL** | Type system / Data loss |
| #2: Race condition in save guard | **HIGH** | Concurrency / Data loss |
| #3: No debouncing on saves | **HIGH** | Performance / Data loss |
| #4: Settings fallback incomplete | **MEDIUM** | Data integrity |
| #5: Categories optional in workspace | **MEDIUM** | Data loss |
| #6: Project reorder logic flawed | **MEDIUM** | User experience |
| #7: Settings field defaults | **LOW** | Code quality |
| #8: SessionStorage guard weak | **LOW** | Edge cases |
| #9: No workspace validation | **LOW** | Robustness |
| #10: OpenTab mapping incomplete | **LOW** | Future-proofing |

---

## File Paths for Reference

- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` - Main persistence logic
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/meta-project-sync.ts` - Symlink syncing
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/stores/workspace.ts` - Client-side state

   115→        newActiveId = null
   116→      }
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    set({ openTabs: newTabs, activeTabId: newActiveId })
   120→  },
   121→
   122→  updateTab: (id, updates) => {
   123→    const { openTabs } = get()
   124→    set({
   125→      openTabs: openTabs.map((t) =>
   126→        t.id === id ? { ...t, ...updates } : t
   127→      )
   128→    })
   129→  },
   130→
   131→  setActiveTab: (id) => set({ activeTabId: id }),
   132→
   133→  clearTabs: () => set({ openTabs: [], activeTabId: null }),
   134→
   135→  setCategories: (categories) => set({ categories }),
   136→
   137→  addCategory: (name) => {
   138→    const { categories } = get()
   139→    const id = generateCategoryId()
   140→    const maxOrder = categories.reduce((max, c) => Math.max(max, c.order), -1)
   141→    set({
   142→      categories: [...categories, { id, name, collapsed: false, order: maxOrder + 1 }]
   143→    })
   144→    return id
   145→  },
   146→
   147→  updateCategory: (id, updates) => {
   148→    const { categories } = get()
   149→    set({
   150→      categories: categories.map((c) =>
   151→        c.id === id ? { ...c, ...updates } : c
   152→      )
   153→    })
   154→  },
   155→
   156→  removeCategory: (id) => {
   157→    const { categories, projects } = get()
   158→    set({
   159→      categories: categories.filter((c) => c.id !== id),
   160→      projects: projects.map((p) =>
   161→        p.categoryId === id ? { ...p, categoryId: undefined } : p
   162→      )
   163→    })
   164→  },
   165→
   166→  reorderCategories: (ids) => {
   167→    const { categories } = get()
   168→    set({
   169→      categories: categories.map((c) => ({
   170→        ...c,
   171→        order: ids.indexOf(c.id)
   172→      }))
   173→    })
   174→  },
   175→
   176→  moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath, categoryId) => {
   177→    const { projects } = get()
   178→    const categoryProjects = projects.filter((p) =>
   179→      categoryId === null ? !p.categoryId : p.categoryId === categoryId
   180→    )
   181→    const maxOrder = categoryProjects.reduce((max, p) => Math.max(max, p.order ?? -1), -1)
   182→    set({
   183→      projects: projects.map((p) =>
   184→        p.path === projectPath
   185→          ? { ...p, categoryId: categoryId ?? undefined, order: maxOrder + 1 }
   186→          : p
   187→      )
   188→    })
   189→  },
   190→
   191→  reorderProjects: (categoryId, projectPaths) => {
   192→    const { projects } = get()
   193→    set({
   194→      projects: projects.map((p) => {
   195→        const index = projectPaths.indexOf(p.path)
   196→        if (index >= 0) {
   197→          return { ...p, order: index }
   198→        }
   199→        return p
   200→      })
   201→    })
   202→  }
   203→}))

     1→import { create } from 'zustand'
     2→import { clearProjectCaches } from '../utils/lruCache'
     3→
     4→export interface ProjectCategory {
     5→  id: string
     6→  name: string
     7→  collapsed: boolean
     8→  order: number
     9→}
    10→
    11→export interface Project {
    12→  path: string
    13→  name: string
    14→  executable?: string
    15→  apiPort?: number
    16→  apiAutoStart?: boolean
    17→  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'
    18→  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'
    19→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
    20→  permissionMode?: string
    21→  color?: string
    22→  ttsVoice?: string
    23→  ttsEngine?: 'piper' | 'xtts'
    24→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    25→  categoryId?: string
    26→  order?: number
    27→}
    28→
    29→export interface OpenTab {
    30→  id: string
    31→  projectPath: string
    32→  sessionId?: string
    33→  title: string
    34→  ptyId: string
    35→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    36→}
    37→
    38→interface WorkspaceState {
    39→  projects: Project[]
    40→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    41→  activeTabId: string | null
    42→  categories: ProjectCategory[]
    43→
    44→  setProjects: (projects: Project[]) => void
    45→  addProject: (project: Project) => void
    46→  removeProject: (path: string) => void
    47→  updateProject: (path: string, updates: Partial<Project>) => void
    48→
    49→  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
    50→  removeTab: (id: string) => void
    51→  updateTab: (id: string, updates: Partial<OpenTab>) => void
    52→  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
    53→  clearTabs: () => void
    54→
    55→  setCategories: (categories: ProjectCategory[]) => void
    56→  addCategory: (name: string) => string
    57→  updateCategory: (id: string, updates: Partial<ProjectCategory>) => void
    58→  removeCategory: (id: string) => void
    59→  reorderCategories: (ids: string[]) => void
    60→  moveProjectToCategory: (projectPath: string, categoryId: string | null) => void
    61→  reorderProjects: (categoryId: string | null, projectPaths: string[]) => void
    62→}
    63→
    64→const generateCategoryId = (): string =>
    65→  `cat-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`
    66→
    67→export const useWorkspaceStore = create<WorkspaceState>((set, get) => ({
    68→  projects: [],
    69→  openTabs: [],
    70→  activeTabId: null,
    71→  categories: [],
    72→
    73→  setProjects: (projects) => set({ projects }),
    74→
    75→  addProject: (project) => {
    76→    const { projects } = get()
    77→    if (!projects.find((p) => p.path === project.path)) {
    78→      set({ projects: [...projects, project] })
    79→    }
    80→  },
    81→
    82→  removeProject: (path) => {
    83→    const { projects } = get()
    84→    clearProjectCaches(path)
    85→    set({ projects: projects.filter((p) => p.path !== path) })
    86→  },
    87→
    88→  updateProject: (path, updates) => {
    89→    const { projects } = get()
    90→    set({
    91→      projects: projects.map((p) =>
    92→        p.path === path ? { ...p, ...updates } : p
    93→      )
    94→    })
    95→  },
    96→
    97→  addTab: (tab) => {
    98→    const { openTabs } = get()
    99→    set({
   100→      openTabs: [...openTabs, tab],
   101→      activeTabId: tab.id
   102→    })
   103→  },
   104→
   105→  removeTab: (id) => {
   106→    const { openTabs, activeTabId } = get()
   107→    const newTabs = openTabs.filter((t) => t.id !== id)

   113→        newActiveId = newTabs[Math.min(index, newTabs.length - 1)].id
   114→      } else {
   115→        newActiveId = null
   116→      }
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    set({ openTabs: newTabs, activeTabId: newActiveId })

   121→
   122→  updateTab: (id, updates) => {
   123→    const { openTabs } = get()
   124→    set({
   125→      openTabs: openTabs.map((t) =>
   126→        t.id === id ? { ...t, ...updates } : t

   128→    })
   129→  },
   130→
   131→  setActiveTab: (id) => set({ activeTabId: id }),
   132→
   133→  clearTabs: () => set({ openTabs: [], activeTabId: null }),
   134→
   135→  setCategories: (categories) => set({ categories }),
   136→

   141→    set({

   143→    })
   144→    return id
   145→  },
   146→
   147→  updateCategory: (id, updates) => {
   148→    const { categories } = get()
   149→    set({

   160→      projects: projects.map((p) =>
